selected_factors: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

overview.summary: Model scored 1 point higher than user. Model emphasized excellent audio/vitals/motion indicators while downweighting GPS displacement. User identified location change as primary distraction factor and reported internal cognitive drift, neither of which the model adequately weighted.

selected_factors: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

factor_sensor_mappings.factor: Internal Cognitive Drift (mind wandering, low motivation)

result.why_difference: Model over-relied on positive audio/vitals/motion signals and under-penalized the 757m GPS displacement despite recognizing it as 'drastic location change.' User explicitly identified location transition as primary distraction and reported cognitive drift (unmeasured by sensors). Model treated GPS anomaly as peripheral rather than central to concentration disruption. The contradiction between stationary motion contexts and large GPS displacement was rationalized away rather than treated as concentration cost.

prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.audio: Highly reliable for this session. 38.68 dB with consistent 'quiet'/'Silence' labels across all 5 events strongly supports low environmental distraction. No recalibration needed; audio correctly indicated favorable conditions.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.vitals: Reliable but incomplete signal. 66.43 bpm heart rate in optimal range and low step count (7.25) support physical calm. However, vitals cannot detect cognitive drift or transition-related mental disruption. Do not over-weight calm vitals when location instability is present.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.motion: Contradictory signal requiring careful interpretation. 4 stationary contexts suggest focused periods, but this conflicts with GPS travel data. Next time, treat stationary contexts as necessary but insufficient for high scores when GPS shows displacement >500m. Motion context may capture micro-periods between transitions rather than session-wide stability.
prediction_agent_sensor_judgment.gps: Under-weighted in current prediction. 757m displacement and 1975m total travel exceeded stability threshold by 3-4x, yet model reduced score only 'slightly.' User confirmed location change as primary distraction. Next cycle: apply stronger penalty (1.5-2 points) for drastic location changes even when other sensors appear favorable. GPS instability should cap score at 7-7.5 maximum regardless of other positive signals.

judgment_policy_next_run: GPS displacement >500m during study sessions should trigger score cap of 7.5 maximum, even with excellent audio/vitals, due to transition costs invisible to other sensors.
When motion shows 'stationary' but GPS shows travel, interpret as fragmented focus across locations rather than continuous concentration. Weight GPS more heavily in such contradictions.
